mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.15% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2019-02-15 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.20%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 3.8% within a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns as equity valuations compress on earnings recession expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% as of 2019-02-15 near cyclical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window following UNRATE threshold breaches.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts fall below 900k units, indicating joint demand weakness across consumer and residential sectors. This interaction signal predicts negative equity returns as forward earnings growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 896k as of 2019-01-15 near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.06 when both conditions are simultaneously active.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling supply-side weakness and potential profit margin compression. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative forward returns as manufacturing weakness spreads to equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100k new jobs per month while unemployment rate remains stable, indicating labor market softening that precedes broader economic slowdown. This interaction signal predicts negative equity returns as forward growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth < 100k AND UNRATE stable within 0.1pp
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 when both conditions are jointly satisfied.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 0.25%, creating a negative real rate environment that initially supports equity valuations through financial asset inflation but signals policy constraint ahead.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 when both conditions are simultaneously active.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 decelerates to below 2% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns. This always-on trend mechanism operates without threshold activation.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of periods when GDPC1 growth is below 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index falls below 98.5 while payroll employment growth declines below 120k monthly, indicating synchronized supply and labor weakness that signals profit margin compression and reduced forward earnings. This interaction mechanism predicts negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 when both conditions are jointly satisfied over any 2-year rolling window.
